chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 3326 of 2023 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge(s) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of main case. 20.12.2024 Syed Shoa-un-Nabi, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Faisal Mahmood Ghani, advocate for the respondent No.1. Ms. Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General. This petitioner seeks employment on the plea of deceased quota. Upon being asked to demonstrate any entitlement in such regard, learned counsel remained unable to assist. Learned counsel for the respondent submits that the petition is barred by laches; as the delay in filing in petition is 21 years. He further states that there is no policy of the respondent for consideration of deceased quota. He submits that the honorable Supreme Court has passed judgment dated 26.09.2024 in the case of General Post Office Islamabad vs. Muhammad Jalal (Civil Petition No.3390 of 2021) and all such indirect recourse to employment has been deprecated. It has also been reported that Rule 11-A of the Sindh Civil Servants (Appointment, Promotion & Transfer) Rules, 1974 has also been deleted by the Sindh Cabinet on 04.12.2024. Reliance was also placed on Supreme Court’s order dated 12.09.2024 in Govt of KPK vs. Tahir Mushtaq (CP 288-P of 2015). In view hereof the petition is found to be patently misconceived, hence, dismissed. Judge Judge Khuhro/PA
